title: [FIRRTL] Fix handling of new-style NLAs in ExtractInstances
body:
Adapt `ExtractInstances` such that it can properly handle the new flavor of NLAs, where non-local annotations would carry a symbol to a hierpath that generally only points at their parent module.

The `ExtractInstances` pass had a few assumptions in its implementation about hierpaths pointing at the annotation target op, and each anno having a unique hierpath op associated with it. The implementation can be simplified quite a bit once support for the old-style NLAs can be ripped out.

This commit also adds explicit tests to check that the old *and* new style of NLAs are working in parallel.

A nasty corner case still exists when extraction requires an NLA to be re-routed because the instance has been extracted past the NLA's original root. If the instance is multiply-extracted, one hierpath gets turned into multiple hierpaths, which will require special handling of *everything* with that hierpath. This commit detects the common case which we can handle and ensures that the hierpath is re-rooted in place, and produces a warning about a potential breakage of hierpath references in the corner case.
